title: docs(quick-start): fix Python OpenAI SDK base_url — add /v1
author: housir2001
pr_body_excerpt:
## Problem

The Quick Start Python example uses:
```python
client = OpenAI(base_url="https://gen.pollinations.ai", api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")
```

This is inconsistent with `APIDOCS.md`, which explicitly states:

> Point the SDK at `https://gen.pollinations.ai/v1` and pass your `sk_…` key as the OpenAI key.

And shows:
```python
client = OpenAI(
    base_url="https://gen.pollinations.ai/v1",
    api_key="sk_your_secret_key",
)
```

The OpenAI Python SDK does **not** auto-append `/v1` to a custom `base_url`. Without `/v1`, requests are constructed incorrectly (e.g. the SDK would target `/chat/completions` instead of `/v1/chat/completions`).

## Fix

Add `/v1` to the `base_url` in the Quick Start Python example to match `APIDOCS.md`.

gist.headline: The missing /v1
gist.blurb: Fixed a sneaky typo in our Python Quick Start guide. The OpenAI SDK now points to the correct /v1 endpoint, so your copy-pasted code will work on the first try.
gist.summary: Added the missing `/v1` suffix to the `base_url` in the Python Quick Start guide so the OpenAI SDK routes requests correctly to `/v1/chat/completions`.
gist.impact: Developers copying the Python example will no longer encounter routing errors when connecting to the text generation API.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee fixing a signpost or a blueprint that has a snake (Python) on it, or adding a 'v1' sticker to a glowing green terminal.
